Today, the neighbouring communes of the metropolitan area (Martinvast, Nouainville, Tonneville, Bretteville, etc.) are experiencing a demographic boost: The framework of life, rural and peaceful, in no way prevents the inhabitants from taking advantage of the infrastructure of the urban community. This problem, which is found in many French towns of this size, has led to the constitution of a [*Pays du Cotentin*](/w/index.php?title=Joint_Association_of_Cotentin&action=edit&redlink=1 "Joint Association of Cotentin (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Syndicat_mixte_du_Cotentin "fr:Syndicat mixte du Cotentin")], the urban community who wish to so participate financially in the rich [Community of communes of Les Pieux](/w/index.php?title=Community_of_communes_of_Les_Pieux&action=edit&redlink=1 "Community of communes of Les Pieux (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Communaut%C3%A9_de_communes_des_Pieux "fr:Communauté de communes des Pieux")] and the [Community of communes of La Hague](/w/index.php?title=Community_of_communes_of_La_Hague&action=edit&redlink=1 "Community of communes of La Hague (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Communaut%C3%A9_de_communes_de_la_Hague "fr:Communauté de communes de la Hague")].

Since the merger between Cherbourg and Octeville, in February 2000, the inhabitants are officially called *Cherbourgeois-Octevillais*.[[65]](#cite_note-fusion-66) Before, the inhabitants of Cherbourg was called the *Cherbourgeois* and those of Octeville were the *Octevillais*. It is likely[*[quantify](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style/Dates_and_numbers "Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Dates and numbers")*] that, with the merger, the latter disappears gradually in favour of *Cherbourgeois*. This would be similar to *Équeurdrevillais* (or sometimes *Équeurdrais*) for the nearby of [Équeurdreville-Hainneville](/wiki/%C3%89queurdreville-Hainneville "Équeurdreville-Hainneville"), which merged communes in 1965.

### Housing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Housing")]

Cherbourg and Octeville have two different profiles. The first is the city centre, with varied habitat, the other a commune in suburbs, built quickly from the 1960s.

### Parks and green spaces

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Parks and green spaces")]

The second half of the 19th century saw the creation of many [English-style gardens](/wiki/English_landscape_garden "English landscape garden"). The first was due to [Joseph Cachin](/wiki/Joseph_Cachin "Joseph Cachin") created while he was responsible for the construction of the port, a private garden and a pond near the [Divette](/w/index.php?title=Divette_(river)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Divette (river) (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Divette_(fleuve) "fr:Divette (fleuve)")], instead of the current railway line leading to the [station](/wiki/Gare_de_Cherbourg "Gare de Cherbourg").[[66]](#cite_note-troisjardins-67) The temperate oceanic climate favours the naturalisation of southern and exotic plants such as palm trees, brought back by many Cherbourg sailors and explorers. Then, under the Third Republic, public gardens opened.[[66]](#cite_note-troisjardins-67)

Today the city offers several green spaces:

* The [Public Garden](/w/index.php?title=Jardin_public_de_Cherbourg&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jardin public de Cherbourg (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jardin_public_de_Cherbourg "fr:Jardin public de Cherbourg")] of 1.7 ha (4.2 acres), on the *Avenue de Paris*, was the first park to be offered to the population, in 1887. At the foot of the *Montagne du Roule*, it hosts many animals ([sea lions](/wiki/Sea_lion "Sea lion"), [aviaries](/wiki/Aviary "Aviary"), [deer](/wiki/Deer "Deer"), etc.). A commemorative site preferred by the municipality, it contains the monument to the dead inaugurated in 1924, the old portal of the Abbey of the Vœu, the bust of [Jean-François Millet](/wiki/Jean-Fran%C3%A7ois_Millet "Jean-François Millet"), and the last town bandstand. Two pavilions of angles constructed in 1889 limit the garden on the *Avenue de Paris*.[[67]](#cite_note-jardinschbg-68)
* The [Emmanuel Liais](/wiki/Emmanuel_Liais "Emmanuel Liais") Park 1 ha (2.5 acres) is the former garden of the Mayor of Cherbourg's house, designed in 1881 and opened in 1885. Bequeathed to the city upon his death, it is very wooded and has an observation tower, a plan of water containing water lilies and other aquatic plants and two greenhouses sheltering rare plants, including a rich collection of South American plants brought back from his travels and acclimated by Liais. It is labelled as a [Remarkable Garden](/wiki/Remarkable_Gardens_of_France "Remarkable Gardens of France").[[68]](#cite_note-69)
* The Montebello garden, opened in 1872 in the street of the same name, within the Napoleon III Quarter, was created at the initiative of the Horticultural Society of Cherbourg for its members. Open to the public since its inception, it contains [bamboos](/wiki/Bamboo "Bamboo"), [camellias](/wiki/Camellia "Camellia") and [magnolias](/wiki/Magnolia "Magnolia"), and offers a chalet of bricks with beams.
* The Park of the [Château des Ravalet](/w/index.php?title=Ch%C3%A2teau_des_Ravalet&action=edit&redlink=1 "Château des Ravalet (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ch%C3%A2teau_des_Ravalet "fr:Château des Ravalet")] 12 ha (30 acres), a Cherbourg-Octeville property on the territory of Tourlaville, was developed by the Vicomte [René de Tocqueville](/w/index.php?title=Ren%C3%A9_de_Tocqueville&action=edit&redlink=1 "René de Tocqueville (page does not exist)") from 1872, with an [English garden](/wiki/English_landscape_garden "English landscape garden") and a woodland. The park and the greenhouse built between 1872 and 1875, which is home to palm, banana, cactus and lianas have been open since the acquisition by the city of Cherbourg in 1935, and are classified as historical monuments since 4 March 1996. Several water bodies welcome Black Swans and the aviaries are home to rare birds. An artificial waterfall was created in 1921.[[67]](#cite_note-jardinschbg-68)
* The [*Vallon sauvage*](/w/index.php?title=Vallon_sauvage&action=edit&redlink=1 "Vallon sauvage (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vallon_sauvage "fr:Vallon sauvage")] [wild valley] contains hedgerows, [wetlands](/wiki/Wetland "Wetland"), orchards and woodland in the heart of Octeville, in a natural area of 10 ha (25 acres).[[10]](#cite_note-pedestre-11)

A private garden, the [Botanical Garden of the Roche Fauconnière](/wiki/Jardin_botanique_de_la_Roche_Fauconni%C3%A8re "Jardin botanique de la Roche Fauconnière"), is also listed in the inventory of Historic Monuments since 29 December 1978.[[69]](#cite_note-70) Established in 1873, it was embellished over generations by the Favier family.[[66]](#cite_note-troisjardins-67)

The commune also has [allotments](/wiki/Allotment_(gardening) "Allotment (gardening)"), managed by associations: Vallon Sauvage, Fourches, Roquettes, Saint Sauveur and Redoute, which gives free land to its members.

In 2007, the municipality was awarded four flowers in the [competition of flowery towns and villages](/wiki/Concours_des_villes_et_villages_fleuris "Concours des villes et villages fleuris").[[70]](#cite_note-71) The beautification policy, which dates from 1995, resulted in obtaining a first flower, followed by a second in 2000 and third in 2002. It relies on public gardens, heirs to a local botanical heritage of over a century, 10,000 square metres (110,000 sq ft) of flower beds and 240 ha (590 acres) of green space on events such as *Le Mois des Jardins et Presqu’île en Fleurs* [The Month of Gardens and Peninsula in Flowers], and the annual distribution of geraniums to the resident volunteers.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Economy")]

### Historic

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Historic")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a4/Cherbourg_AncienneCri%C3%A9e.jpg/250px-Cherbourg_AncienneCri%C3%A9e.jpg)](/wiki/File:Cherbourg_AncienneCri%C3%A9e.jpg)

Old fish market of Cherbourg, *Quai de Caligny*.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8f/Cherbourg-arsenal_hangars.jpg/250px-Cherbourg-arsenal_hangars.jpg)](/wiki/File:Cherbourg-arsenal_hangars.jpg)

Sheds of the arsenal, seen from Chantereyne.

At the instigation of [Colbert](/wiki/Jean-Baptiste_Colbert "Jean-Baptiste Colbert"), the guild of drapers was founded on 16 April 1668, the manufacture of cloth produced two thousand pieces per year.[[53]](#cite_note-anecdotique-54) Two years earlier, Colbert had also promoted the introduction of the [glass factory](/wiki/La_Glacerie "La Glacerie") in the forest of [Tourlaville](/wiki/Tourlaville "Tourlaville").[[71]](#cite_note-72)

In the 18th century, the economic resources came mainly through maritime trade, the preparation of cured meats and the harbour and breakwater works, plus a moribund textile industry. On the eve of the French Revolution, salt was imported from [Le Croisic](/wiki/Le_Croisic "Le Croisic") along with British grain, and [Littry](/wiki/Le_Molay-Littry "Le Molay-Littry") coal. Exports were mainly to Britain (sheets and clothes) and the West Indies (cattle and mules, fat and salted butter, salted meats, cod, linens and canvas), but also to [Le Havre](/wiki/Le_Havre "Le Havre") and [La Rochelle](/wiki/La_Rochelle "La Rochelle") for wood and coal. Lawful or otherwise exchanges also took place with the Channel Islands ([tanbark](/wiki/Tanbark "Tanbark"), grain and wool). Cherbourg shipowners were absent from significant fishing, including that of cod on the banks of [Newfoundland](/wiki/Newfoundland_(island) "Newfoundland (island)"), which was a specialty of [Granville](/wiki/Granville,_Manche "Granville, Manche"). 361 workers (1764) and 69 skilled workers (1778) of the factory annually produced (1760) 2,000 fine linens in green and white strip. Cherbourg also had seven producers of starch.[[44]](#cite_note-ancienregime-45) Opened in 1793 at the location of the current Lawton-Collins Wharf, the arsenal was moved in 1803 on a decision by [Napoleon](/wiki/Napoleon "Napoleon"), within the project of the [military port](/wiki/Cherbourg_Naval_Base "Cherbourg Naval Base"). Sailing ships were built, the first, the [brig](/wiki/Brig "Brig") *La Colombe*, was launched on 27 September 1797, and then screw-propelled vessels up to the end of the 19th century. From 1898, the Arsenal specialised in the [construction of submarines](/w/index.php?title=List_of_submarines_built_at_Cherbourg&action=edit&redlink=1 "List of submarines built at Cherbourg (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liste_des_sous-marins_construits_%C3%A0_Cherbourg "fr:Liste des sous-marins construits à Cherbourg")]. The first were *Le Morse* and *Le Narval*. Since then, more than 91 vessels have been built there.

*L'Annuaire de la Manche* [The Yearbook of Manche] in 1829 mentioned several slate quarries in the agglomeration whose product was sometimes exported to Le Havre, two printers, two soda refineries (properties of Mr. Le Couturier and Messrs. Crenier and Co. producing approximately 600 tonnes for [Ostend](/wiki/Ostend "Ostend"), [Dunkirk](/wiki/Dunkirk "Dunkirk"), [Rouen](/wiki/Rouen "Rouen") and [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), Germany and Russia), a sugar refinery (Mr. Despréaux) whose 50 tonnes were sold in the English Channel, a lace factory run by four nuns on behalf of Messrs. Blod and Lange and several tanners. It is indicated that the port trade was based on exportation of mules to [Réunion](/wiki/R%C3%A9union "Réunion") and the [Antilles](/wiki/Antilles "Antilles"), salted meat of pigs and eggs in [Britain](/wiki/Great_Britain "Great Britain"), wine and brandies, and the import of [Scandinavian](/wiki/Scandinavia "Scandinavia"), [Polish](/wiki/Poland "Poland") and Russian wood, linseed, and hemp.[[72]](#cite_note-73) But its use as a place of war hampered the development of Cherbourg as major commercial port, compared to Le Havre. Ten years later, for these exchanges, [Jean Fleury](/w/index.php?title=Jean_Fleury_(writer)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jean Fleury (writer) (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jean_Fleury "fr:Jean Fleury")] counted 225 to 230 both French and foreign, from 30 to 800 tons, ships each carrying 6 to 18 crew. He added the maritime buildings and armaments and the export of butter of [La Hague](/wiki/La_Hague "La Hague"), and the total annual trade was estimated at between 4 or 5 million francs, of which one million for the export of eggs to the United Kingdom, and 850 tons of salted meat.[[55]](#cite_note-fleury1839-notions-56)

At the beginning of the 20th century, Cherbourg was primarily a [military port](/wiki/Cherbourg_Naval_Base "Cherbourg Naval Base"). The commercial port was modest, always exporting mules to the West Indies and Réunion and local food products to Britain (butter, meats, eggs, cattle, etc.), but also chemical products of soda extracted from kelp, granite from nearby quarries, and important wood and iron from [Nord](/wiki/Nord_(French_department) "Nord (French department)"), tar, hemp, and food from the colonies. At this time the port embraced the transatlantic epic. Cherbourg's industry was then specialised in shipbuilding, as well as in lace-making and the manufacture of rope. The late 19th century also saw Cherbourg develop an aviation industry, through the company of [Félix du Temple](/wiki/F%C3%A9lix_du_Temple_de_la_Croix "Félix du Temple de la Croix"), taken over in 1938 by [Félix Amiot](/wiki/F%C3%A9lix_Amiot "Félix Amiot"), another aviation pioneer for the aerospace company of Normandy. Gradually, workers developed a particular skill in metalwork, both for the submarines of the Arsenal, for aircraft and ships of the Amiot shipyards or [Babcock](/wiki/George_Herman_Babcock "George Herman Babcock")-[Wilcox](/wiki/Stephen_Wilcox "Stephen Wilcox") boilers.[[73]](#cite_note-74)

In 1916, [Nestlé](/wiki/Nestl%C3%A9 "Nestlé") introduced its first French factory in Cherbourg.

The 1960s saw a revival of the local economy through the increase in the female workforce and the decline of agricultural employment in favour of diversification of jobs and a high-tech industry. In 1960, under the leadership of Mayor [Jacques Hébert](/wiki/Jacques_H%C3%A9bert_(French_politician) "Jacques Hébert (French politician)"), Hortson was established in the Maupas quarter. One hundred employees manufactured projectors and film cameras, particularly for the [ORTF](/wiki/Office_de_Radiodiffusion_T%C3%A9l%C3%A9vision_Fran%C3%A7aise "Office de Radiodiffusion Télévision Française") and Russian television. Redeemed, the factory specialised under the name of [Thomson-CSF](/wiki/Thomson-CSF "Thomson-CSF") audiovisual in surveillance and medical cameras, then in the production of electronic circuits of computer terminals on behalf of [Constructions Mécaniques de Normandie](/wiki/Constructions_M%C3%A9caniques_de_Normandie "Constructions Mécaniques de Normandie") and the Arsenal. Since 1976, it has been dedicated to the production of microwave electronic devices, employing 260 workers in 1979 contracted for radars of the [Mirage F1](/wiki/Dassault_Mirage_F1 "Dassault Mirage F1") Army Air and of the Navy [Super Etendards](/wiki/Dassault-Breguet_Super_%C3%89tendard "Dassault-Breguet Super Étendard"), rising to 400 employees at the end of the 1980s, after moving in 1987 into a new modernised factory in Tourlaville. For a decade, the electronic workshop expanded, adding a production line for mobile television relays, and a workshop for mechanical surface treatment.[[74]](#cite_note-Hortson-75) As part of the internal restructuring of [Alcatel](/wiki/Alcatel-Lucent "Alcatel-Lucent"), the site, which has 300 employees, was sold in 2002 to [Sanmina-SCI](/wiki/Sanmina_Corporation "Sanmina Corporation"), which ceased its activity in March 2008.[[75]](#cite_note-redynamiser-76) The *Compagnie industrielle des télécommunications* (CIT), merged the following decade with Alcatel, it also opened an assembly plant for electronic telephone exchanges, at [Querqueville](/wiki/Querqueville "Querqueville") in the 1960s. The unit, seen as a flagship of French industry by the new president of the Republic in 1981, was considered unnecessary after the integration of Thomson's telephony division with Alcatel in 1984 and suffered heavy redundancies from the end of the 1980s, before closing in 1997 at the end of a difficult social conflict.[[76]](#cite_note-77)

Between the 1970s and 1990s, the two major projects of northern [Cotentin](/wiki/Cotentin_Peninsula "Cotentin Peninsula"), the [La Hague reprocessing plant](/wiki/La_Hague_site "La Hague site") and the [Flamanville Nuclear Power Plant](/wiki/Flamanville_Nuclear_Power_Plant "Flamanville Nuclear Power Plant"), accentuated the industrial development of a city that saw a golden age[[51]](#cite_note-cite-inquiete-52) through what the journalist François Simon called "industries of death", since about two thirds of the local industrial fabric was related to defence and the nuclear industry.[[50]](#cite_note-histoireindustrielle-51)

Cherbourg is also the cradle of the [Halley](/wiki/Paul-Louis_Halley "Paul-Louis Halley") family and society, which became [Promodès](/wiki/Promod%C3%A8s "Promodès") in the 1960s ([Continent](/w/index.php?title=Continent_(hypermarket)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Continent (hypermarket) (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Continent_(hypermarch%C3%A9) "fr:Continent (hypermarché)")] hypermarkets, [Champion](/wiki/Champion_(supermarket) "Champion (supermarket)") supermarkets). In 1999, Promodès merged with [Carrefour](/wiki/Carrefour "Carrefour"). The old buildings of Halley House became the technical centre of the Cachin vocational school, on *Avenue Aristide-Briand*.

### Economic data

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Economic data")]

In 1999, the economically active population of Cherbourg and Octeville was 18,671 inhabitants in a total population of 42,288 inhabitants.[[77]](#cite_note-linternaute-78)

Cherbourg-Octeville supports an unemployment rate (19.6% in 1999), double that of its job base (9.3% in 2006, a decrease of 1.1% in one year) which itself has the highest unemployment of the basins of employment of the department. At 31 December 2004, there were 3,700 jobseekers. Therefore, the annual average household income is lower than the national average (€13,730 for the city, compared with €15,027 in France) despite an average monthly salary (€1,590 in 2001) highest job growth of the department and higher than that of [Caen](/wiki/Caen "Caen")-[Bayeux](/wiki/Bayeux "Bayeux") (€1,550).[[17]](#cite_note-memo-18)[[77]](#cite_note-linternaute-78)

### Main activities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=30 "Edit section: Main activities")]

Cherbourg is the seat of the [Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Cherbourg-Cotentin](/w/index.php?title=Chambre_de_commerce_et_d%27industrie_de_Cherbourg-Cotentin&action=edit&redlink=1 "Chambre de commerce et d'industrie de Cherbourg-Cotentin (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chambre_de_commerce_et_d%27industrie_de_Cherbourg-Cotentin "fr:Chambre de commerce et d'industrie de Cherbourg-Cotentin")] particularly manages the airport, the fishing ports of Cherbourg and the trade, and, together with the [Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Centre and Sud-Manche](/w/index.php?title=Chambre_de_commerce_et_d%27industrie_de_Centre_et_Sud-Manche&action=edit&redlink=1 "Chambre de commerce et d'industrie de Centre et Sud-Manche (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chambre_de_commerce_et_d%27industrie_de_Centre_et_Sud-Manche "fr:Chambre de commerce et d'industrie de Centre et Sud-Manche")], the FIM group training organisation.

* **Maritime sector**

Further information: [fr:Port de Cherbourg](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Port_de_Cherbourg "fr:Port de Cherbourg")

The Cherbourg economy derives a large part of its activities from its maritime position.[[79]](#cite_note-boncap-80) Cherbourg indeed has [four ports](/w/index.php?title=Port_of_Cherbourg&action=edit&redlink=1 "Port of Cherbourg (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Port_de_Cherbourg "fr:Port de Cherbourg")]: A military port, a fishing port, a port of commerce (passenger traffic and cross-border goods) and a marina.

Weakened since the 1990s, the commercial port sees the transit of 110,000 trucks to or from Ireland and Great Britain. [Project Fastship](/wiki/FastShip,_Inc. "FastShip, Inc."), involving container transport from [Philadelphia](/wiki/Philadelphia "Philadelphia") (United States) by high-speed vessels and oped for fifteen years, has been forgotten in favour of the [Motorways of the Sea](/wiki/Motorways_of_the_Sea "Motorways of the Sea") in the context of the Ena (Eurocoast Network Association), with [Cuxhaven](/wiki/Cuxhaven "Cuxhaven") (Germany), [Ostend](/wiki/Ostend "Ostend") (Belgium), [Rosslare](/wiki/Rosslare_Europort "Rosslare Europort") (Ireland) and [Ferrol](/wiki/Ferrol,_Galicia "Ferrol, Galicia") (Spain), with no more effect at the moment.[[75]](#cite_note-redynamiser-76)

In recent years, the cross-Channel passenger traffic has declined, with competition from [Caen-Ouistreham](/wiki/Port_of_Caen "Port of Caen") and the [Pas-de-Calais](/wiki/Pas-de-Calais "Pas-de-Calais"). The withdrawal of the [P&O company](/wiki/P%26O_Portsmouth "P&O Portsmouth"), which served Poole and Southampton, has left two companies with cross-Channel links: [Brittany Ferries](/wiki/Brittany_Ferries "Brittany Ferries") to [Portsmouth](/wiki/Portsmouth "Portsmouth") and [Poole](/wiki/Poole "Poole") and [Irish Ferries](/wiki/Irish_Ferries "Irish Ferries") to [Rosslare](/wiki/Rosslare_Europort "Rosslare Europort") (Ireland). In the first eleven months of 2007 compared with the same period of 2006, passenger traffic declined by 3.84% to 750,000 units, while freight fell 4.43% with 87,000 trucks landed. For comparison, the port had 1.7 million passengers and 138,000 trucks in 1995.[[80]](#cite_note-retroport-81)

Property, with the [Port of Caen-Ouistreham](/wiki/Port_of_Caen "Port of Caen"), of the joint association Ports Norman Associates, involving the [Regional Council of Lower Normandy](/w/index.php?title=Regional_Council_of_Lower_Normandy&action=edit&redlink=1 "Regional Council of Lower Normandy (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conseil_r%C3%A9gional_de_Basse-Normandie "fr:Conseil régional de Basse-Normandie")] and the Departmental Councils of [Manche](/wiki/Departmental_Council_of_Manche "Departmental Council of Manche") and [Calvados](/wiki/Departmental_Council_of_Calvados "Departmental Council of Calvados"), port trade is managed by a joint company of the [Chamber of commerce](/w/index.php?title=Chamber_of_Commerce_and_Industry_of_Cherbourg-Cotentin&action=edit&redlink=1 "Chamber of Commerce and Industry of Cherbourg-Cotentin (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chambre_de_commerce_et_d%27industrie_de_Cherbourg-Cotentin "fr:Chambre de commerce et d'industrie de Cherbourg-Cotentin")] and [Louis Dreyfus Armateurs](/wiki/Louis_Dreyfus_Armateurs "Louis Dreyfus Armateurs") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Louis_Dreyfus_Armateurs "fr:Louis Dreyfus Armateurs")]. The construction of a terminal dedicated to the traffic of coal from South America and destined for the United Kingdom will put an end to the haemorrhage of the activity of the port.[[81]](#cite_note-82)

The fishing industry is affected by the crisis affecting the entire industry, and the port has seen its fleet decline.[[75]](#cite_note-redynamiser-76)

Cherbourg was the first French marina by number of visitors in 2007, having 10,117 boats for 28,713 overnight stays in 2007, and the total impact estimated at €4 million for the Cherbourg agglomeration.[[82]](#cite_note-83)

A tradition of local industry, shipbuilding is based on the two pillars of the [DCNS](/wiki/DCNS_(company) "DCNS (company)") Cherbourg for [submarines](/wiki/Submarine "Submarine") and [Constructions Mécaniques de Normandie](/wiki/Constructions_M%C3%A9caniques_de_Normandie "Constructions Mécaniques de Normandie") (CMN), famous for their speedboats. This sector has been widely restructured over the past twenty years. The military arsenal saw the end of the construction of the [*Redoutable*-class submarines](/wiki/Redoutable-class_submarine_(1967) "Redoutable-class submarine (1967)") and expanded its customer base, until then exclusively of the Navy, prior to being privatised in 2007. With diesel *Agosta* submarines, developed since 1994 for Pakistan, and the *Scorpène*, in collaboration with the shipyards of [Cartagena](/wiki/Cartagena,_Spain "Cartagena, Spain"), sold to Malaysia, Chile and India, 25% of the total turnover of the establishment is of foreign origin. Partnerships with Pakistan and India have concluded to make the construction term at home. The CMN, which employed 1,200 people at the beginning of the 1980s, modernised and automated, and now has 500 employees. The company diversified into large luxury yachts, without abandoning the military market, and has signed such contracts with the [United Arab Emirates](/wiki/United_Arab_Emirates "United Arab Emirates") and [Qatar](/wiki/Qatar "Qatar") through the Franco-[Lebanese](/wiki/Lebanon "Lebanon") businessman [Iskandar Safa](/wiki/Iskandar_Safa "Iskandar Safa"), owner since 1992.[[79]](#cite_note-boncap-80)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/37/TransatJ.Vabre6_11_2005_BankPop.jpg/250px-TransatJ.Vabre6_11_2005_BankPop.jpg)](/wiki/File:TransatJ.Vabre6_11_2005_BankPop.jpg)

Multihull *Banque Populaire*, built by the [JMV Industries](/w/index.php?title=JMV_Industries&action=edit&redlink=1 "JMV Industries (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/JMV_Industries "fr:JMV Industries")] shipyard

While these two military companies have experienced reductions in loads (the number of jobs at the Arsenal increased from 6,000 including 1,000 subcontracted, in 1988, to 2,600 including 500 subcontractors), and the companies have repositioned in the nautical industry. [JMV Industries](/w/index.php?title=JMV_Industries&action=edit&redlink=1 "JMV Industries (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/JMV_Industries "fr:JMV Industries")], a subsidiary of CMN with 100 employees, built racing yachts. Originally hosted by CMN to build aluminium hulls designed by James Ébénistes ([Saint-Laurent-de-Cuves](/wiki/Saint-Laurent-de-Cuves "Saint-Laurent-de-Cuves")), Allures Yachting has specialised in cruising sailboats. The Allais shipyard, of Dieppe, has established a subsidiary, ICAN, dedicated to civilian boats and pleasure craft.[[79]](#cite_note-boncap-80)

A network of subcontractors and specialists formed around this hub through Ameris France (established in 1994 under the name of *Cap 50 export*, specialised in the research and the supply of spare parts for ships and military aircraft), the Efinor group (founded in 1988, specialising in metallurgy, nuclear decommissioning and engineering), MPH (help in project control, 140 employees). At [Saint-Vaast-la-Hougue](/wiki/Saint-Vaast-la-Hougue "Saint-Vaast-la-Hougue"), Facnor has become a global specialist of sailing reels.[[83]](#cite_note-84)

The Navy employs nearly 3,000 officials in the agglomeration, especially in the context of administration (maritime prefecture), maritime safety (customs, CROSS, Abeille), logistical support of the [French Navy](/wiki/French_Navy "French Navy") and foreign passage, and of training.[[84]](#cite_note-85)

* **Metallurgy**

Metallurgy has long represented a large source of employment in the agglomeration. Around the Arsenal and its [boilermakers](/wiki/Boilermaker "Boilermaker"), several metalworking and mechanical industries were formed from the early 1900s. This is the case of the oldest business the city, the Simon Brothers company, founded in 1856, which went from being a mechanical workshop to a steam agricultural machinery manufacturer and then to an agribusiness in a half a century.

Manufacturing guns in 1870 and 1939, the company became a world leader in [churns](/wiki/Butter_churn "Butter churn") and mixers for the butter industry.[[85]](#cite_note-86) Similarly, the Babcock [boiler](/wiki/Boiler "Boiler") manufacturer was implanted in Cherbourg in the [interwar period](/wiki/Interwar_period "Interwar period") and closed its doors after a protracted labour dispute, in 1979. Later, the UIE began business in Cherbourg in 1973, for the construction of [oil platforms](/wiki/Oil_platform "Oil platform"), but closed in 1985.[[50]](#cite_note-histoireindustrielle-51)

* **Agri-food**

The food industry, essential in Lower Normandy, is not absent from the employment pool. A farm raising salmon in the harbour, abattoirs handling farmed livestock of Nord-Cotentin, and several processing companies exist. The Simon Brothers (50 employees) have supplied equipment for the cider and dairy industries for more than a century.

* **Electronics**

Alcatel had two units in the 1980s, one in Cherbourg, then Tourlaville (formerly [Thomson-CSF](/wiki/Thomson-CSF "Thomson-CSF")) the other in Querqueville ([Alcatel CIT](/wiki/Alcatel-Lucent "Alcatel-Lucent")). Both, regarded as flagships of the group, specialised respectively in microwave and electronic telephone exchanges. However, Alcatel decided to close the Querqueville factory in 1997, Codifur then took over part of the business with hundreds of employees. In 2002, it also offloaded the Tourlaville unit to [Sanmina-SCI](/wiki/Sanmina_Corporation "Sanmina Corporation"), which relocated its production six years later. Codifur resumed the after-sales service business of Alcatel, or 5% of the initial activity, and a few dozen employees.[[86]](#cite_note-87)

* **Other industries**[[87]](#cite_note-tresor-88)

Socoval, a manufacturer of menswear of the Cantoni Group from Italy, is the last textile factory of the Cotentin and employs about 100 employees, since the social plan of 2001, which resulted in the loss of about 40 employees.

Economic partners now rely on the "mastery of atmosphere", i.e. the control of contamination from industrial processes, through the [Cherbourg-Normandy technopole](/w/index.php?title=Technopole_Cherbourg_Normandie&action=edit&redlink=1 "Technopole Cherbourg Normandie (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Technopole_Cherbourg_Normandie "fr:Technopole Cherbourg Normandie")] created in 2001. Having experience of work involving nuclear risk, it wants to transfer these skills to the food, electronics and pharmaceutical industries. Two courses have been designed for this purpose: A BTS in nuclear maintenance at the Lycee Tocqueville and a DESS in mastery of atmosphere at the [Cherbourg School of Engineering](/wiki/Cherbourg_School_of_Engineering "Cherbourg School of Engineering").

* **Commerce**

The urban community, the main commercial centre of the Cotentin, has four [hypermarkets](/wiki/Hypermarket "Hypermarket") covering 26,780 m2 (288,300 sq ft)[[23]](#cite_note-memo2008-24) - of which one, [Carrefour](/wiki/Carrefour "Carrefour") (260 employees), located in the Cherbourg area, represents the third largest private employer of the commune - as well as several large specialist stores. Trade employs nearly 1,400 people in the city centre,[[87]](#cite_note-tresor-88) but the decline in cross-Channel traffic has caused a big shortfall, exacerbated by the fragile local economy.[[75]](#cite_note-redynamiser-76)
Although downtown Cherbourg is the main commercial centre of the agglomeration, with 340 establishments, its dominance is lower in the urban community, when compared to Caen towards its agglomeration. Indeed, Cherbourg focuses 35% of commercial activities and 45% of retail trade in the agglomeration, against 40% and 55% for the centre of Caen respectively, particularly two-thirds of the human equipment stores against 90% in Lower Normandy's capital. Grocery chains, equipment and home appliances have left the city centre for out-of-town shopping centres. The number of fast food outlets doubled between 1995 and 2005, while the strength of the traditional catering has stagnated.[[88]](#cite_note-89)

* **Services**[[87]](#cite_note-tresor-88)

Cherbourg-Octeville, the largest city of the department, is the main centre for administration and services for the [Cotentin](/wiki/Cotentin "Cotentin"). Health is an important provider of jobs with the [Pasteur hospital](/w/index.php?title=Centre_hospitalier_public_du_Cotentin&action=edit&redlink=1 "Centre hospitalier public du Cotentin (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Centre_hospitalier_public_du_Cotentin "fr:Centre hospitalier public du Cotentin")] (470 beds, second of Lower Normandy, merged since 2006 with the Hospital of Valognes) and the Cotentin Polyclinic. The same goes for the education sector with four public and four private schools, a marine high school and aquaculture, a university campus and several graduate schools. The branches of public enterprises are also located there (EDF, with 120 officers and SNCF, with 50 officers). Public employment represents an important part with, in addition to the hospital and schools, municipal and community staff.

Business service companies are also present in computer science (Euriware, 85 employees), cleanliness (Onet, 240 employees, and Sin&Stes, 100 employees) and advertising (Adrexo, 50 employees).

Cherbourg-Octeville hosts the headquarters of [France Bleu Cotentin](/w/index.php?title=France_Bleu_Cotentin&action=edit&redlink=1 "France Bleu Cotentin (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/France_Bleu_Cotentin "fr:France Bleu Cotentin")] public radio, and the departmental daily *[La Presse de la Manche](/wiki/La_Presse_de_la_Manche "La Presse de la Manche")* (120 employees with his CES press), successor to the Libération de [*Cherbourg-Éclair*](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg-%C3%89clair&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cherbourg-Éclair (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cherbourg-%C3%89clair "fr:Cherbourg-Éclair")], and subsidiary of the [Groupe SIPA - Ouest-France](/w/index.php?title=Groupe_SIPA_-_Ouest-France&action=edit&redlink=1 "Groupe SIPA - Ouest-France (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groupe_SIPA_-_Ouest-France "fr:Groupe SIPA - Ouest-France")] since 1990. [France 3 Normandie](/wiki/France_3_Normandie "France 3 Normandie") boasts a local editorial office in the city; Cherbourg's edition of [*La Manche libre*](/w/index.php?title=La_Manche_libre&action=edit&redlink=1 "La Manche libre (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/La_Manche_libre "fr:La Manche libre")] covers the agglomeration, [La Hague](/wiki/La_Hague "La Hague") and the [Val de Saire](/wiki/Val_de_Saire "Val de Saire"); local television [5050 TV](/w/index.php?title=5050_TV&action=edit&redlink=1 "5050 TV (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/5050_TV "fr:5050 TV")] has installed its headquarters and its main studio in the area.

Jobs in the construction sector are divided between Faucillion (80 employees), [Eiffage](/wiki/Eiffage "Eiffage") (75 employees) and [Colas](/wiki/Colas_Group "Colas Group") (60 employees).

Since its opening, the *[Cité de la Mer](/wiki/Cit%C3%A9_de_la_Mer "Cité de la Mer")* is the tourist engine of Nord-Cotentin. The cruise terminal also attracts liners each year. The marina of 1,500 spaces is the first French port of call (11,000 per year). The capacity of the city was, as of 1 January 2007, 15 hotels and 429 rooms. The casino, owned by the Cogit Group is the 109th in France, with a turnover of €6.7 million.[[87]](#cite_note-tresor-88)

## Politics and administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: Politics and administration")]

In 2010, the commune of Cherbourg-Octeville was awarded a 3-star equivalent "[Ville Internet](/w/index.php?title=Ville_Internet&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ville Internet (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ville_Internet "fr:Ville Internet")]" [label](/wiki/Certification_mark "Certification mark")[[89]](#cite_note-90) and was upgraded to a 4-star equivalent rating in 2012.

### Administrative divisions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Administrative divisions")]

The city has the [central office](/w/index.php?title=Bureau_centralisateur&action=edit&redlink=1 "Bureau centralisateur (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bureau_centralisateur "fr:Bureau centralisateur")] of two cantons: [Canton of Cherbourg-Octeville-1](/wiki/Canton_of_Cherbourg-Octeville-1 "Canton of Cherbourg-Octeville-1") (to the west) and [Cherbourg-Octeville-2](/wiki/Canton_of_Cherbourg-Octeville-2 "Canton of Cherbourg-Octeville-2") (in the east, which also includes the town of La Glacerie). [Departmental advisors](/w/index.php?title=List_of_general_councillors_of_Manche&action=edit&redlink=1 "List of general councillors of Manche (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liste_des_conseillers_g%C3%A9n%C3%A9raux_de_la_Manche "fr:Liste des conseillers généraux de la Manche")] are the [Socialists](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(France) "Socialist Party (France)") Frédéric Bastian, Anna Pic, Karine Duval and Sébastien Fagnen.

The [arrondissement of Cherbourg](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Cherbourg "Arrondissement of Cherbourg") has 189 municipalities and 190,363 inhabitants. The sub-prefect is Jacques Troncy, former sub-prefect of [Montbéliard](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Montb%C3%A9liard "Arrondissement of Montbéliard"), appointed 17 March 2014.[[90]](#cite_note-91)

Since 1986, the [fifth constituency of Manche](/w/index.php?title=Fifth_constituency_of_Manche&action=edit&redlink=1 "Fifth constituency of Manche (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cinqui%C3%A8me_circonscription_de_la_Manche "fr:Cinquième circonscription de la Manche")], known as Cherbourg, covered the three cantons of Cherbourg-Octeville, and those of [Equeurdreville-Hainneville](/wiki/Canton_of_%C3%89queurdreville-Hainneville "Canton of Équeurdreville-Hainneville"), [Saint-Pierre-Église](/w/index.php?title=Canton_of_Saint-Pierre-%C3%89glise&action=edit&redlink=1 "Canton of Saint-Pierre-Église (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canton_de_Saint-Pierre-%C3%89glise "fr:Canton de Saint-Pierre-Église")] and [Tourlaville](/wiki/Canton_of_Tourlaville "Canton of Tourlaville"). In the context of the legislative redistricting of 2010, the two cantons of [Beaumont-Hague](/wiki/Beaumont-Hague "Beaumont-Hague") and [Quettehou](/wiki/Quettehou "Quettehou") integrated the constituency of Cherbourg-Octeville, becoming the [4th constituency](/wiki/Manche%27s_4th_constituency "Manche's 4th constituency"). Despite this redistribution often perceived as advantageous for the right, the outgoing [Socialist](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(France) "Socialist Party (France)") deputy of the 5th constituency, [Bernard Cazeneuve](/wiki/Bernard_Cazeneuve "Bernard Cazeneuve"), was re-elected in the first round with 55% of the vote.

Cherbourg-Octeville also has the headquarters of the [maritime prefecture](/wiki/Maritime_Prefect "Maritime Prefect") of the [English Channel](/wiki/English_Channel "English Channel") and the [North Sea](/wiki/North_Sea "North Sea"), whose authority extends from the Bay of [Mont Saint-Michel](/wiki/Mont_Saint-Michel "Mont Saint-Michel") to the [Belgian](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") border. The [maritime prefect](/w/index.php?title=List_of_maritime_prefects_of_Cherbourg&action=edit&redlink=1 "List of maritime prefects of Cherbourg (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liste_des_pr%C3%A9fets_maritimes_de_Cherbourg "fr:Liste des préfets maritimes de Cherbourg")] is the Vice Admiral of the Squadron, [Bruno Nielly](/w/index.php?title=Bruno_Nielly&action=edit&redlink=1 "Bruno Nielly (page does not exist)"). The [maritime quarter](/wiki/Home_port "Home port") of Cherbourg (initials: CH) is restricted to the limits of the department.

Since 1971, Cherbourg-Octeville has belonged to the [Urban community of Cherbourg](/w/index.php?title=Urban_community_of_Cherbourg&action=edit&redlink=1 "Urban community of Cherbourg (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Communaut%C3%A9_urbaine_de_Cherbourg "fr:Communauté urbaine de Cherbourg")], presided over by [André Rouxel](/wiki/Andr%C3%A9_Rouxel "André Rouxel") since 2012, Mayor of Tourlaville, to which the municipality delegates urban transport, management of space and the quality of life, the environment and development strategies (higher education, research, major projects, and *Cité de la Mer*, etc.).

The postal codes from prior to the merger of 2000 have been preserved: 50130 for addresses of the former territory of Octeville, 50100 for Cherbourg.

### Political trends and results

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Political trends and results")]

Further information: [fr:Élections municipales de 2014 dans la Manche](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%89lections_municipales_de_2014_dans_la_Manche "fr:Élections municipales de 2014 dans la Manche")

Cherbourg is historically, with the Arsenal and the port, the main focus of labour and trades unions of the department of Manche. However, the Cherbourg workers do not lean towards radical or revolutionary movements, nor to [yellow unionism](/wiki/Company_union "Company union"), traditionally preferring the reformist tendencies. These choices are reflected politically into a strong center-left anchor, dominated by Socialist-radicals and independent Socialists, before whom the [SFIO](/wiki/French_Section_of_the_Workers%27_International "French Section of the Workers' International") and the [Socialist Party](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(France) "Socialist Party (France)") are not imposed.[[91]](#cite_note-92) Since the Liberation, with the exception of a Gaullist period of 18 years with [Jacques Hébert](/wiki/Jacques_H%C3%A9bert_(French_politician) "Jacques Hébert (French politician)") following [René Schmitt's](/w/index.php?title=Ren%C3%A9_Schmitt&action=edit&redlink=1 "René Schmitt (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ren%C3%A9_Schmitt "fr:René Schmitt")] death, the city of Cherbourg has voted in favour Socialist forces.

Similarly, the right won the town hall of Octeville in 1989, for a term, by the division of the left. Since the redrawing of the electoral district of Cherbourg in 1986, covering the urban population of the agglomeration and the rural district of the [Canton of Saint-Pierre-Église](/w/index.php?title=Canton_of_Saint-Pierre-%C3%89glise&action=edit&redlink=1 "Canton of Saint-Pierre-Église (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canton_de_Saint-Pierre-%C3%89glise "fr:Canton de Saint-Pierre-Église")], the left-right alternation is the rule in every legislative election.

**Presidential elections, results of the second rounds**

* [Election of 2012](/wiki/2012_French_presidential_election "2012 French presidential election")[[92]](#cite_note-93) (77.28% participation): 60.00% for [François Hollande](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Hollande "François Hollande") ([PS](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(France) "Socialist Party (France)"), elected), 40.00% for [Nicolas Sarkozy](/wiki/Nicolas_Sarkozy "Nicolas Sarkozy") ([UMP](/wiki/Union_for_a_Popular_Movement "Union for a Popular Movement")).
* [Election of 2007](/wiki/2007_French_presidential_election "2007 French presidential election")[[93]](#cite_note-94) (83.27% participation): 52.03% for [Ségolène Royal](/wiki/S%C3%A9gol%C3%A8ne_Royal "Ségolène Royal") ([PS](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(France) "Socialist Party (France)")), 47.97% for Nicolas Sarkozy ([UMP](/wiki/Union_for_a_Popular_Movement "Union for a Popular Movement"), elected).
* [Election of 2002](/wiki/2002_French_presidential_election "2002 French presidential election")[[94]](#cite_note-95) (76.82% participation): 86,81% for [Jacques Chirac](/wiki/Jacques_Chirac "Jacques Chirac") ([RPR](/wiki/Rally_for_the_Republic "Rally for the Republic"), elected), 13.19% for [Jean-Marie Le Pen](/wiki/Jean-Marie_Le_Pen "Jean-Marie Le Pen") ([FN](/wiki/National_Front_(France) "National Front (France)")).

**Parliamentary elections, results of the second rounds (1 round, unique for 2012)**

* [Elections of 2012](/wiki/2012_French_legislative_election "2012 French legislative election")[[95]](#cite_note-96) (54.48% participation), candidates who have collected more than 5% of the vote: 56.57% to [Bernard Cazeneuve](/wiki/Bernard_Cazeneuve "Bernard Cazeneuve") ([PS](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(France) "Socialist Party (France)"), elected), 39.23% David Margueritte ([UMP](/wiki/Union_for_a_Popular_Movement "Union for a Popular Movement")), 8.80% for Jean-Jacques Christmas (FN), 5.55% for Ralph Lejamtel ([EELV](/wiki/Europe_Ecology_%E2%80%93_The_Greens "Europe Ecology – The Greens")).
* [Elections of 2007](/wiki/2007_French_legislative_election "2007 French legislative election")[[96]](#cite_note-97) (61.28% participation): 60.77% for Bernard Cazeneuve (PS, elected), 39.23% [Jean Lemière](/w/index.php?title=Jean_Lemi%C3%A8re&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jean Lemière (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jean_Lemi%C3%A8re "fr:Jean Lemière")] (UMP).
* [Elections of 2002](/wiki/2002_French_legislative_election "2002 French legislative election")[[97]](#cite_note-98) (62.49% participation): 50.78% for Bernard Cazeneuve (PS), 49.22% for Jean Lemière (UMP, elected).

**European elections, results of the two scores or more than 15%**

* [2014 election](/wiki/2014_European_Parliament_election_in_France "2014 European Parliament election in France")[[98]](#cite_note-99) (41.70% participation): 20.59% for [Jérôme Lavrilleux](/w/index.php?title=J%C3%A9r%C3%B4me_Lavrilleux&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jérôme Lavrilleux (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/J%C3%A9r%C3%B4me_Lavrilleux "fr:Jérôme Lavrilleux")] ([UMP](/wiki/Union_for_a_Popular_Movement "Union for a Popular Movement")), 20.38% for [Gilles Pargneaux](/wiki/Gilles_Pargneaux "Gilles Pargneaux") ([PS](/wiki/Socialist_Party_(France) "Socialist Party (France)")-[PRG](/wiki/Radical_Party_of_the_Left "Radical Party of the Left")), 19.85% for [Marine Le Pen](/wiki/Marine_Le_Pen "Marine Le Pen") ([FN](/wiki/National_Front_(France) "National Front (France)")).
* [2009 election](/wiki/2009_European_Parliament_election_in_France "2009 European Parliament election in France") (37.63% participation): 24.98% for [Dominique Riquet](/wiki/Dominique_Riquet "Dominique Riquet") (UMP), 22.51% for Gilles Pargneaux (PS), 14.2% for [Hélène Flautre](/wiki/H%C3%A9l%C3%A8ne_Flautre "Hélène Flautre") ([Europe Écologie](/wiki/Europe_%C3%89cologie "Europe Écologie")).
* [2004 election](/wiki/2004_European_Parliament_election_in_France "2004 European Parliament election in France")[[99]](#cite_note-100) (40.81% participation): 37.75% for [Henri Weber](/wiki/Henri_Weber "Henri Weber") (PS), 16.74% for [Tokia Saïfi](/wiki/Tokia_Sa%C3%AFfi "Tokia Saïfi") (UMP).

**Regional elections**

### List of mayors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=34 "Edit section: List of mayors")]

Further information: [fr:Liste des maires de Cherbourg](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liste_des_maires_de_Cherbourg "fr:Liste des maires de Cherbourg") and [fr:Liste des maires d'Octeville](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liste_des_maires_d%27Octeville "fr:Liste des maires d'Octeville")

With the merging of the municipal councils of Cherbourg and Octeville on 1 March 2000, [Jean-Pierre Godefroy](/wiki/Jean-Pierre_Godefroy "Jean-Pierre Godefroy") (PS), the Mayor of Cherbourg, took the helm of the new administration, and [Bernard Cazeneuve](/wiki/Bernard_Cazeneuve "Bernard Cazeneuve") (PS), Mayor of Octeville, became the first Deputy. Bernard Cazeneuve was elected Mayor of Cherbourg-Octeville during the [2001 municipal election](/wiki/2001_French_municipal_elections "2001 French municipal elections"), and re-elected in March 2008 with 66.82% of the vote. Appointed Minister Delegate for European Affairs in May 2012, he gave way to Jean-Michel Houllegatte the following month. The latter was re-elected following the victory of his list with 51.81% at the second round of the [2014 municipal election](/wiki/2014_French_municipal_elections "2014 French municipal elections").

### Municipal administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=35 "Edit section: Municipal administration")]

The [municipal council](/wiki/City_council_(France) "City council (France)") is composed of 39 members including the mayor and eleven assistants.[[114]](#cite_note-of-municipales2014-115) Thirty Councillors represent a leftist majority, nine represent the opposition.[[114]](#cite_note-of-municipales2014-115)

### Budget

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Budget")]

The main initial budget for 2007 amounted to €73,994,364, divided between the operating section (€54,126,712) and investment section (€19,867,652).[[115]](#cite_note-budget-116) Personnel expenses exceeded half (60%) of operating expenses. Almost all of the resources were fuelled by grants (49%) and tax (44%).[[116]](#cite_note-recettes-117) Of the seven budgets of the municipal mandate (2001-2007), this budget increased overall by 22% (43% for investments, 15% for operation).[[117]](#cite_note-118)

The investment budget is included in the multiannual programme "Unite the city" (2003-2007), presented in December 2002 by Bernard Cazeneuve. It sees a transformation of the newly amalgamated city through refurbishment of the pool and Port Chantereyne, development of the Bassins zone, filling the retaining channel, and the construction of the sailing school.[[118]](#cite_note-119) It is mainly financed by borrowing, increasing the debt of the city (the charges multiplied by two-thirds between 2002 and 2007), lower than the average per capita of the stratum.[[119]](#cite_note-120)

### Justice

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=38 "Edit section: Justice")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/43/Cherbourg_maison_d%27arret.JPG/250px-Cherbourg_maison_d%27arret.JPG)](/wiki/File:Cherbourg_maison_d%27arret.JPG)

The remand prison of Cherbourg.

Louis XVI removed the viscountcy of Cherbourg by edict in November 1771, and transferred the rights of justice to the Bailiwick of Valognes.[[125]](#cite_note-126) In 1785, a [sub-delegation](/w/index.php?title=Subd%C3%A9l%C3%A9gation&action=edit&redlink=1 "Subdélégation (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Subd%C3%A9l%C3%A9gation "fr:Subdélégation")] was created, however it was smaller than the Viscountcy, essentially covering [La Hague](/wiki/La_Hague "La Hague"), as far as [Héauville](/wiki/H%C3%A9auville "Héauville") and included Helleville, Tourlaville, and Bretteville, Digosville and Martinvast, and always under the authority of the electoral district of Valognes.

Cherbourg became a town and district in January 1790, seat of a justice of the peace and a civil and criminal court. Under the Directory, they were replaced by a simple police court, attended by a justice of the peace, and a police correctional court. The correctional court closed after the Act of 17 February 1800 and Cherbourg was made a town and township of the [Arrondissement of Valognes](/w/index.php?title=Arrondissement_of_Valognes&action=edit&redlink=1 "Arrondissement of Valognes (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arrondissement_de_Valognes "fr:Arrondissement de Valognes")]. After his arrival in the city, [Napoleon](/wiki/Napoleon "Napoleon") established a [Court of first instance](/wiki/Trial_court "Trial court") by the Decree of 19 July 1811.[[126]](#cite_note-127)

The city is today one of the three main judicial hubs of Manche, along with [Coutances](/wiki/Coutances "Coutances") and [Avranches](/wiki/Avranches "Avranches"). It hosts a [high court](/w/index.php?title=Tribunal_de_grande_instance_(France)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tribunal de grande instance (France) (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tribunal_de_grande_instance_(France) "fr:Tribunal de grande instance (France)")] (arrondissement of Cherbourg-Octeville), a [court](/wiki/Tribunal_d%27instance "Tribunal d'instance") (Canton of Beaumont-Hague, Cherbourg-Octeville-Nord-Ouest, Cherbourg-Octeville-Sud-Est, Équeurdreville-Hainneville, Cherbourg-Octeville-Sud-Ouest, Les Pieux, Saint-Pierre-Eglise and Tourlaville), a [council of tribunals](/wiki/Labour_Court_(France) "Labour Court (France)") and a [tribunal of commerce](/wiki/Tribunal_de_commerce "Tribunal de commerce"). A [remand prison](/wiki/Maison_d%27arr%C3%AAt "Maison d'arrêt") is located in the city centre, behind the buildings of the court. As a result of the justice reform presented in 2007 by Rachida Dati, the jurisdiction of the Court of Valognes will be integrated to that of Cherbourg.

The Engineering School of Cherbourg, on the main university campus.

Two [ZEP](/w/index.php?title=Zone_d%27%C3%A9ducation_prioritaire&action=edit&redlink=1 "Zone d'éducation prioritaire (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zone_d%27%C3%A9ducation_prioritaire "fr:Zone d'éducation prioritaire")] have been defined, one being on Cherbourg territory in the Quarter of Maupas, the other straddling Cherbourg and Octeville, in the quarter of the Provinces.

Cherbourg-Octeville has six high schools:

* The former college, which became high school in 1886, is known by the name of Lycée Victor Grignard (830 students: General and TSG, as well as [scientific preparatory classes](/w/index.php?title=Classes_pr%C3%A9paratoires_scientifiques&action=edit&redlink=1 "Classes préparatoires scientifiques (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Classes_pr%C3%A9paratoires_scientifiques "fr:Classes préparatoires scientifiques")]).
* The Lycée Jean-François Millet (1,210 students: General sector, preparatory health, [preparatory literary classes](/wiki/Kh%C3%A2gne "Khâgne")).
* The Lycée Alexis de Tocqueville (1,480 students: General sector, technical, professional and higher education -BTS-).
* The Ingénieur-Cachin private professional high school (320 students).
* The Thomas Hélye private high school, the Sainte-Chantal private lycée and the La Bucaille technological lycée (1,005 students, courses) general and technical.
* The aquaculture and marine high school (102 pupils). On 28 June 2013, this became the professional maritime and aquaculture lycée [Daniel Rigolet](/w/index.php?title=Daniel_Rigolet&action=edit&redlink=1 "Daniel Rigolet (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Daniel_Rigolet "fr:Daniel Rigolet")].

The university campus, installed on the heights of Octeville, focuses the [Cherbourg School of Engineering](/wiki/Cherbourg_School_of_Engineering "Cherbourg School of Engineering"), the [IUT Cherbourg-Manche](/w/index.php?title=IUT_Cherbourg-Manche&action=edit&redlink=1 "IUT Cherbourg-Manche (page does not exist)") (which hosts approximately 1,000 students in initial or continuing education through four DUT departments, four pro licenses, one DU, a DCEF and a DAEU), as well as two branches of the [University of Caen](/wiki/University_of_Caen_Lower_Normandy "University of Caen Lower Normandy") (UFR sciences and UFR modern foreign languages). The Pasteur hospital houses the Institute of training in nursing of Cherbourg-Octeville. The Group FIM, training service of the two [chambers of commerce and industry](/wiki/Chamber_of_commerce "Chamber of commerce") of la Mancha, manages the school of trade and distribution, and since 2007, the Institute of promotion and marketing boating, forming alternating a fortnight of accreditation in the field of boating (shipbuilding, nautical services company, marinas, etc.).

The Institute of the Film Industry of Normandy is installed as a result of the [International School of Audiovisual Creation and Realisation](/wiki/%C3%89cole_Internationale_de_Cr%C3%A9ation_Audiovisuelle_et_de_R%C3%A9alisation "École Internationale de Création Audiovisuelle et de Réalisation") (EICAR) on the site of the former [maritime hospital](/w/index.php?title=H%C3%B4pital_des_arm%C3%A9es_Ren%C3%A9-Le-Bas&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hôpital des armées René-Le-Bas (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%B4pital_des_arm%C3%A9es_Ren%C3%A9-Le-Bas "fr:Hôpital des armées René-Le-Bas")]. It is complemented by the training of apprentices of the Performing Arts and Audiovisual Centre, while the [School of Fine Arts](/w/index.php?title=%C3%89cole_sup%C3%A9rieure_d%27arts_et_m%C3%A9dias_de_Caen_-_Cherbourg&action=edit&redlink=1 "École supérieure d'arts et médias de Caen - Cherbourg (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%89cole_sup%C3%A9rieure_d%27arts_et_m%C3%A9dias_de_Caen_-_Cherbourg "fr:École supérieure d'arts et médias de Caen - Cherbourg")] (Esbaco), founded in 1912 by Henri Buffet design Professor and Professor of modelling Félix Delteil, is located in the former convent of the [Little Sisters of the Poor](/wiki/Little_Sisters_of_the_Poor "Little Sisters of the Poor") (Bassins zone) since 1976.[[128]](#cite_note-129)

Cherbourg-Octeville welcomes the [School of the Military Applications of Atomic Energy](/w/index.php?title=School_of_the_military_applications_of_Atomic_Energy&action=edit&redlink=1 "School of the military applications of Atomic Energy (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%89cole_des_applications_militaires_de_l%27%C3%A9nergie_atomique "fr:École des applications militaires de l'énergie atomique")] (EAMEA, 351 students) and the [National Institute of Science and Nuclear Technology](/wiki/Institut_national_des_sciences_et_techniques_nucl%C3%A9aires "Institut national des sciences et techniques nucléaires") - remains of the importance of the army in the city - while the School of the Quartermasters (between 600 and 700 students) is located at [Querqueville](/wiki/Querqueville "Querqueville").

The town also hosts the [National Institute of Science and Technology of the Sea](/w/index.php?title=National_Institute_of_Science_and_Technology_of_the_Sea&action=edit&redlink=1 "National Institute of Science and Technology of the Sea (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Institut_national_des_sciences_et_techniques_de_la_mer "fr:Institut national des sciences et techniques de la mer")] (Intechmer), Tourlaville.

### Sport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: Sport")]

The first horse race organised in Normandy took place in Cherbourg in September 1836 on the (now gone) beachfront along the *boulevard maritime*, at the initiative of [Éphrem Houël](/w/index.php?title=%C3%89phrem_Hou%C3%ABl&action=edit&redlink=1 "Éphrem Houël (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/%C3%89phrem_Hou%C3%ABl "fr:Éphrem Houël")], the officer of the stud. The races settled in 1931 on the Lande Saint-Gabriel Racecourse in Tourlaville, the work of [René Levavasseur](/w/index.php?title=Ren%C3%A9_Levavasseur_(architect)&action=edit&redlink=1 "René Levavasseur (architect) (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ren%C3%A9_Levavasseur_(architecte) "fr:René Levavasseur (architecte)")], and the [Hippodrome de La Glacerie](/w/index.php?title=Hippodrome_de_La_Glacerie&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hippodrome de La Glacerie (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hippodrome_de_La_Glacerie "fr:Hippodrome de La Glacerie")] from 1990.[[129]](#cite_note-130)

In [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"), [AS Cherbourg Football](/wiki/AS_Cherbourg_Football "AS Cherbourg Football"), after decades at the national level, is changing, as a result of bad sports results and financial problems, since 2014 in the [Ligue de Basse-Normandie de football](/w/index.php?title=Ligue_de_Basse-Normandie_de_football&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ligue de Basse-Normandie de football (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ligue_de_Basse-Normandie_de_football "fr:Ligue de Basse-Normandie de football")], equivalent to the 6th division, and is based at the [Maurice-Postaire stadium](/w/index.php?title=Stade_Maurice-Postaire&action=edit&redlink=1 "Stade Maurice-Postaire (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stade_Maurice-Postaire "fr:Stade Maurice-Postaire")]. The club has also two other senior male teams in the League of Lower Normandy.[[130]](#cite_note-131)

Three other clubs have teams in [district divisions](/w/index.php?title=District_d%C3%A9partemental_de_football&action=edit&redlink=1 "District départemental de football (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/District_d%C3%A9partemental_de_football "fr:District départemental de football")]:[[131]](#cite_note-132)

* The *Patronage laïque d'Octeville* [Secular Patronage of Octeville] (three teams)
* The *Association sportive de l'Arsenal maritime de Cherbourg* [Sporting Association of the Marine Arsenal of Cherbourg] (one team)
* Gazélec Football Club (two teams)

The Association sportive Amont-Quentin, which had two district teams until June 2013,[[132]](#cite_note-133) had to cease its activities. The Octeville Hague Sport, which was developing its teams in 2013–2014, was unable to present any team for the 2014–2015 season.[[133]](#cite_note-134)

In cycling, Cherbourg has been a city of arrival for the [Tour de France](/wiki/Tour_de_France "Tour de France") on sixteen occasions: 1911 to 1914 (four consecutive years), from 1919 to 1929 (eleven consecutive years), and finally in [1986](/wiki/1986_Tour_de_France "1986 Tour de France"). Cherbourg was a departure city in [1994](/wiki/1994_Tour_de_France "1994 Tour de France"). The second stage of the [2016 Tour de France](/wiki/2016_Tour_de_France "2016 Tour de France") finishes at Cherbourg.[[134]](#cite_note-135)

Cherbourg regularly hosts stages of sailing competitions such as the [Solitaire du Figaro](/wiki/Solitaire_du_Figaro "Solitaire du Figaro"), the [Course de l'Europe](/w/index.php?title=Course_de_l%27Europe&action=edit&redlink=1 "Course de l'Europe (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Course_de_l%27Europe "fr:Course de l'Europe")], the [Challenge Mondial Assistance](/w/index.php?title=Challenge_Mondial_Assistance&action=edit&redlink=1 "Challenge Mondial Assistance (page does not exist)"), the [Tour de France à la voile](/wiki/Tour_de_France_%C3%A0_la_voile "Tour de France à la voile"), and the [Tall Ships' Race](/wiki/Tall_Ships%27_Races "Tall Ships' Races").

The [Challenger La Manche](/wiki/Challenger_La_Manche "Challenger La Manche") is a professional tennis tournament ($50,000 plus accommodation) held annually in Cherbourg since 1994.

In ice hockey, NC'HOP ([Nord Cotentin Hockey Plus](/w/index.php?title=Nord_Cotentin_Hockey_Plus&action=edit&redlink=1 "Nord Cotentin Hockey Plus (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nord_Cotentin_Hockey_Plus "fr:Nord Cotentin Hockey Plus")], the *Cherbourg Vikings*) was based in the city. The team was the successor to CHOC (Cherbourg Hockey Club), who played in [FFHG Division 1](/wiki/FFHG_Division_1 "FFHG Division 1") until filing for bankruptcy in 1996. NC'HOP left the competition in 2010.

AS Cherbourg men's basketball team plays in [National 2](/w/index.php?title=Championnat_de_France_de_basket-ball_de_Nationale_masculine_2&action=edit&redlink=1 "Championnat de France de basket-ball de Nationale masculine 2 (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Championnat_de_France_de_basket-ball_de_Nationale_masculine_2 "fr:Championnat de France de basket-ball de Nationale masculine 2")] and the women's team in the Prenational league.

The [Jeunesse sportive de Cherbourg](/w/index.php?title=Jeunesse_sportive_de_Cherbourg&action=edit&redlink=1 "Jeunesse sportive de Cherbourg (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jeunesse_sportive_de_Cherbourg "fr:Jeunesse sportive de Cherbourg")] has a male handball team in [Pro D2](/w/index.php?title=Championnat_de_France_de_handball_masculin_de_Division_2&action=edit&redlink=1 "Championnat de France de handball masculin de Division 2 (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Championnat_de_France_de_handball_masculin_de_Division_2 "fr:Championnat de France de handball masculin de Division 2")] and a women's team in [Nationale 3](/w/index.php?title=Championnat_de_France_de_handball_f%C3%A9minin_de_Nationale_3&action=edit&redlink=1 "Championnat de France de handball féminin de Nationale 3 (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Championnat_de_France_de_handball_f%C3%A9minin_de_Nationale_3 "fr:Championnat de France de handball féminin de Nationale 3")] (agreement with [Tourlaville](/wiki/Tourlaville "Tourlaville")). The club has also implemented a structured training programme for young people, from the 2009/2010 season, around a partnership with colleges of the city.

### Health

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=42 "Edit section: Health")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fb/CHPC_Cherbourg1.jpg/250px-CHPC_Cherbourg1.jpg)](/wiki/File:CHPC_Cherbourg1.jpg)

The main front of the Pasteur Hospital.

Cherbourg-Octeville has two hospitals:

* The Pasteur Central Hospital, public, second establishment of Lower Normandy with 711 beds and places.[[135]](#cite_note-136) With a joint medical project since 2001 and a communal direction since 2003, the Pasteur Centre and the Central Hospital of Valognes merged in 2006 within the [Centre hospitalier public du Cotentin](/w/index.php?title=Centre_hospitalier_public_du_Cotentin&action=edit&redlink=1 "Centre hospitalier public du Cotentin (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Centre_hospitalier_public_du_Cotentin "fr:Centre hospitalier public du Cotentin")] (2,000 officials, more than 1,000 beds, and a 2005 budget of €133 million)[[136]](#cite_note-137)
* The Polyclinic of the Cotentin, at the border between Octeville and [Équeurdreville-Hainneville](/wiki/%C3%89queurdreville-Hainneville "Équeurdreville-Hainneville") (102 beds).

The Gros Hêtre medicalised residence for elderly (a branch of the public hospital in the Cotentin) and, since 1999, the Jean-Brüder Community Health Centre are located on the territory of Octeville.

In 1859, following the Imperial visit, the State decided upon the construction of a [Maritime Hospital](/w/index.php?title=H%C3%B4pital_des_arm%C3%A9es_Ren%C3%A9-Le-Bas&action=edit&redlink=1 "Hôpital des armées René-Le-Bas (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%B4pital_des_arm%C3%A9es_Ren%C3%A9-Le-Bas "fr:Hôpital des armées René-Le-Bas")] of a thousand beds to accommodate the troops in garrison. Opened on 15 February 1869, it was renamed René-Le-Bas, named after the first doctor who joined the Free Naval Forces and died in 1942 aboard the submarine [*Surcouf*](/wiki/French_submarine_Surcouf "French submarine Surcouf"). It was closed in 2002 and renovated as a university campus.

### Religion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Cherbourg&action=edit&section=43 "Edit section: Religion")]

Attached to the [Diocese of Coutances-Avranches](/wiki/Roman_Catholic_Diocese_of_Coutances "Roman Catholic Diocese of Coutances"), until 1 September 2009 the deanery of Cherbourg covered the territory of the urban community and the bordering communes of [Tonneville](/wiki/Tonneville "Tonneville"), [Urville-Nacqueville](/wiki/Urville-Nacqueville "Urville-Nacqueville"), and those of the former [Communauté de communes de la Saire](/wiki/Communaut%C3%A9_de_communes_de_la_Saire "Communauté de communes de la Saire"), and the [Communauté de communes de Douve et Divette](/wiki/Communaut%C3%A9_de_communes_de_Douve_et_Divette "Communauté de communes de Douve et Divette"). To this date, it is merged to the deanery of [La Hague](/wiki/La_Hague "La Hague"), to become the [Deanery of Cherbourg-Hague](/w/index.php?title=Deanery_of_Cherbourg-Hague&action=edit&redlink=1 "Deanery of Cherbourg-Hague (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Doyenn%C3%A9_de_Cherbourg-Hague "fr:Doyenné de Cherbourg-Hague")], therefore adding the parishes covering the [Community of communes of La Hague](/w/index.php?title=Communaut%C3%A9_de_communes_de_la_Hague&action=edit&redlink=1 "Communauté de communes de la Hague (page does not exist)") [[fr](https://fr.wikipedia.org/wiki/Communaut%C3%A9_de_communes_de_la_Hague "fr:Communauté de communes de la Hague")] and of [Les Pieux](/wiki/Communaut%C3%A9_de_communes_des_Pieux "Communauté de communes des Pieux"). Jean-XXIII parish unites Cherbourg and La Glacerie, with the Cherbourg churches of La Trinité (long only parish church), Notre-Dame-du-Roule, Notre-Dame-du-Vœu, St-Jean-des-Carrières and Saint-Clément. The parish of Saint-Sauveur of Octeville, which also covers [Nouainville](/wiki/Nouainville "Nouainville") has three sites in the commune: Saint-Martin, the historic site, Saint-Pierre-Saint-Paul, at Provinces, and the Chapel of Saint-Barthélemy.[[137]](#cite_note-138)

Protestants have a temple of the [Reformed Church](/wiki/Reformed_Church_of_France "Reformed Church of France") (since 1835, rebuilt after the war in 1964), and a [Pentecostal](/wiki/Pentecostalism "Pentecostalism") [Evangelical](/wiki/Evangelicalism "Evangelicalism") church affiliated with the [Assemblies of God](/wiki/Assemblies_of_God "Assemblies of God"). The Evangelical [Baptist](/wiki/Baptists "Baptists") Church has been present since 1985 in the agglomeration and is currently located in Tourlaville.

Cherbourg and Octeville experienced two major waves of immigration of [Muslim](/wiki/Islam "Islam") population, the late 1950s and during the 1960s, after the construction of the neighbourhoods of Amont-Quentin, Provinces and Maupas, and then in the 1980s, with the major construction sites of the [La Hague reprocessing site](/wiki/La_Hague_site "La Hague site") and the [Flamanville Nuclear Power Plant](/wiki/Flamanville_Nuclear_Power_Plant "Flamanville Nuclear Power Plant"). The Muslim community then opened three mosques (the Mosque of Omar in Octeville, the Mosque de la Gare, on the *Avenue de Normandie*, and then the Turkish mosque, on the *Boulevard de l’Atlantique*).

[The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints](/wiki/The_Church_of_Jesus_Christ_of_Latter-day_Saints "The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints"), Parish of Cherbourg, has its chapel on the *Rue du Commerce*.
